channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Small plane skids off runway at Oregon airport, ends up in bay
item.description: A small private plane attempting to land at an Oregon airport skidded off the runway and into a body of water, leaving 5 people injured.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Foran, Fox 5

item.title: Trump is expected to sign executive orders to boost coal, a reliable but polluting energy source
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump is expected to sign executive orders Tuesday aimed at boosting coal, a reliable but polluting energy source that’s long been in decline.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW DALY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Former Sen. Joe Manchin is working on a memoir. 'Dead Center' is scheduled for September
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Former Sen. Joe Manchin has a memoir coming out in September, what the self-styled maverick from West Virginia calls his “declaration of independence from the extremes on both sides.” St.
item.dc:creator: HILLEL ITALIE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Kentucky watches for surging rivers to recede so widespread cleanup can begin
item.description: FRANKFORT, Ky. (AP) — After days of unrelenting downpours swelled rivers to near record levels across Kentucky, residents closely monitored waterways Tuesday for signs they had crested, but freezing temperatures could complicate any cleanup efforts.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER and KRISTIN M. HALL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Scholarships for child care are drying up. Now families are paying the price
item.description: PHOENIX (AP) — For parents who need to work but can't afford the steep cost of child care, federally funded scholarships can be a lifeline. Delivered through state child care assistance programs, the scholarships can mean the difference between a parent working full time — or not at all.
item.dc:creator: MORIAH BALINGIT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Reversible damage found in marathon runners' brains: study
item.description: A 26.2-mile run can do some damage to the brain, a study shows, but the impact is temporary and reversible.
item.dc:creator: Ashley N. Soriano, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Idaho mom who killed 2 of her kids goes on trial over husband's death
item.description: Lori Vallow Daybell, the Idaho mother with doomsday religious beliefs who was convicted of killing her two youngest children and conspiring to murder a romantic rival, is on trial again. This time, she's accused in Arizona of conspiring to murder her estranged husband.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Man claims to be ICE officer, threatens to deport victims, deputies say
item.description: When deputies tracked the suspect down, he turned the story on the victims, claiming he was threatened with deportation.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, NewsNation

item.title: Miami’s ‘Little Venezuela’ fears Trump's moves against migration
item.description: DORAL, Fla. (AP) — Wilmer Escaray left Venezuela in 2007 and enrolled at Miami Dade College, opening his first restaurant six years later.
item.dc:creator: GISELA SALOMON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US stocks dip after careening through a manic day following Trump's latest tariff threat
item.description: U.S. stocks swung wildly in a manic Monday after President Donald Trump doubled down on his tariffs, despite seeing how much Wall Street wants him to do the opposite.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: US stocks dip after careening through a manic day following Trump's latest tariff threat
item.description: U.S. stocks swung wildly in a manic Monday after President Donald Trump doubled down on his tariffs, despite seeing how much Wall Street wants him to do the opposite.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: RFK Jr. says MMR vaccine 'most effective' way to stop spread of measles after Texas child's death
item.description: Kennedy, who has a history of vaccine skepticism, made the remarks after visiting the family of an unvaccinated 8-year-old girl who died from the disease.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KRON4

item.title: FAFSA has struggled for years. Then Trump cut the Education Department in half
item.description: Families and students are growing nervous about the fate of the Free Application for Federal Student Aid amid President Donald Trump's planned cuts to the Department of Education.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: Trump tariffs pose danger for vulnerable Republicans
item.description: Vulnerable House Republicans are being put on defense amid the fallout from President Donald Trump’s sweeping global tariffs, which have stoked uncertainty about the economy.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, KRON4

item.title: Trump says he's not backing down, calls tariffs 'medicine' as markets reel
item.description: Speaking to reporters aboard Air Force One, Trump said he didn't want global markets to fall, but also that he wasn't concerned about the massive sell-off either.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Israel controls 50% of Gaza after razing land to expand its buffer zone
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israel has dramatically expanded its footprint in the Gaza Strip since relaunching its war against Hamas last month. It now controls more than 50% of the territory and is squeezing Palestinians into shrinking wedges of land.
item.dc:creator: SAM MEDNICK, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Most cars have this button, but few know when to use it
item.description: An often overlooked button on your car or truck's dashboard could be saving you some sweat and money.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: No state has axed its income tax on wages in 45 years. Now 2 Southern states are on a path to do so
item.description: About 45 years have passed since a U.S. state last eliminated its income tax on wages and salaries. But with recent actions in Mississippi and Kentucky, two states now are on a path to do so, if their economies keep growing.
item.dc:creator: DAVID A. LIEB, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: California town reports third fatality related to rare virus linked to death of Gene Hackman's wife
item.description: Investigators recently blamed the rare virus for the death of Betsy Arakawa, the wife of actor Gene Hackman.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Protesters from New York to Alaska assail Trump and Musk in 'Hands Off!' rallies
item.description: Crowds of people angry about the way President Donald Trump is running the country marched and rallied in scores of American cities Saturday in the biggest day of demonstrations yet by an opposition movement trying to regain its momentum after the shock of the Republican’s first weeks in office.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Jaguar and Land Rover carmaker pauses shipments to US over Trump tariffs
item.description: The company that makes Jaguar and Land Rover vehicles said on Saturday it would pause shipments to the U.S., days after President Donald Trump’s tariffs took effect.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Stellantis joins Ford in offering employee discounts to public
item.description: The move follows in the steps of Ford Motor Company, which announced a similar bargain earlier this month.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: What to know about the severe storms and flash flooding hitting parts of the US
item.description: LITTLE ROCK, Ark. (AP) — Parts of the South and Midwest, still reeling from violent storms, tornadoes and flooding that have killed more than a dozen people, face new flooding and tornado warnings that forecasters said could last for days.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DeMILLO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: At least 16 dead in flooding and tornadoes as storms slash from Texas to Ohio
item.description: DYERSBURG, Tennessee. (AP) — Another round of torrential rain and flash flooding came Saturday for parts of the South and Midwest already heavily waterlogged by days of severe storms that also spawned deadly tornadoes. Forecasters warned that rivers in some places would continue to rise for days.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER and OBED LAMY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Tariffs will make sneakers, jeans and almost everything Americans wear cost more, trade groups warn
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Sending children back to school in new sneakers, jeans and T-shirts is likely to cost U.S. families significantly more this fall if the bespoke tariffs President Donald Trump put on leading exporters take effect as planned, American industry groups warn.
item.dc:creator: ANNE D'INNOCENZIO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Storms tore up two of America's most iconic trails. Federal cuts have disrupted repairs
item.description: CAMPO, Calif. (AP) — Hiking the Pacific Crest Trail is a challenge, especially for adventurers making the entire run from Southern California to Canada, and Eric Kipperman’s job is to greet them at the start and lay bare the difficulties ahead.
item.dc:creator: JULIE WATSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Justice Department places attorney who struggled to explain Maryland man's deportation on leave
item.description: A Justice Department attorney who struggled in court on Friday to explain the Trump administration's deportation of a Maryland man to El Salvador has been placed on administrative leave, a department official said.
item.dc:creator: By Ken Dilanian, Gary Grumbach and Raquel Coronell Uribe, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump goes all in with bet that the heavy price of tariffs will pay off for Americans
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Not even 24 hours after his party lost a key Wisconsin race and underperformed in Florida, President Donald Trump followed the playbook that has defined his political career: He doubled down. Trump’s move Wednesday to place stiff new tariffs on imports from nearly all U.S.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER and STEVE PEOPLES, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Al Green says he’ll present articles of impeachment against Trump in next 30 days
item.description: Green, who vowed to bring articles of impeachment against Trump roughly two months ago, argued that Trump does not “deserve the office” he holds.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WGN 9

item.title: Increasing sleepiness linked to dementia risk in women, study finds
item.description: The research by Neurology discovered women in their eighties who experience sleep disruption have an association with cognitive aging.
item.dc:creator: Diana Falzone, ABC 4

item.title: Protesters tee off against Trump and Musk in 'Hands Off!' rallies across the US
item.description: Crowds of people angry about the way President Donald Trump is running the country marched and rallied in scores of American cities Saturday in the biggest day of demonstrations yet
item.dc:creator: Dave Collins, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump administration argues judge can't order return of man mistakenly deported to El Salvador
item.description: A federal judge didn't have the authority to order the Trump administration to broker the return of a Maryland man who was mistakenly deported from the U.S. to a notorious El Salvador prison, government attorneys argued Saturday as they urged an appeals court to suspend the ruling.
item.dc:creator: Fox 5

item.title: Man claims to be ICE agent, threatens to deport victims, deputies say
item.description: A Florida man is accused of pulling a vehicle over, claiming to be an ICE agent, and threatening to deport two victims.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, Fox 5

item.title: Zelenskyy meets European military leaders to plan for a peacekeeping force
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy met the leaders of the British and French armed forces in Kyiv Saturday to discuss the potential deployment of a multinational peacekeeping force to Ukraine, despite the reluctance of U.S. President Donald Trump to provide security guarantees.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Cleveland's José Ramírez hits 3 homers and claims another piece of Guardians history
item.description: José Ramírez hit three homers and carried the Guardians to a victory Friday night. The slugger claimed another place in Cleveland baseball history in the process.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Are Trump’s tariffs a negotiating tool? Depends on whom you ask
item.description: Businesses, consumers and foreign leaders are trying to assess just how set in stone President Trump’s tariffs are, as the administration and its allies send mixed signals about whether the measures are being used for leverage.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Pentagon watchdog to review Hegseth’s use of Signal app to convey plans for Houthi strike
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Pentagon’s acting inspector general announced Thursday that he would review Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth’s use of the Signal messaging app to convey plans for a military strike against Houthi militants in Yemen.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, ABC 4
